calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-03-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jayleen Woodbury for Petitioner via Courtcall.

The Court asked counsel about the Vanguard issue. Ms. Woodbury stated that the letter in exhibit E explains that Vanguard got an attorney, that when Vanguard returned the funds it was a mistake, that a second check was sent to Vanguard and that check has been cashed. Ms. Woodbury further stated that only the petitioner's funds are left, that the petitioner is the only beneficiary, that minor errors addressed by Probate Examiner such as typo have been fixed and hopes to get the petition approved today. In addition, Ms. Woodbury stated that the funds have properly gone back to Vanguard, that Vanguard has disclosed the beneficiary as Kari Baldwin (sp) also known as Kari Bobkowski, that she spoke with her over the phone and told her to contact Vanguard for the funds, and that Kari Baldwin has been noticed formally and over the phone. The Court noted the accounting petition is under submission and inquired if Ms. Woodbury is aware of any oppositions. Ms. Woodbury stated she is not aware of any opposition, that she has noticed any possible living relative to let them know what was going on with the case, that the decedent's daughter/petitioner is the only beneficiary of the estate other than the Vanguard funds, and the few minor errors does not affect the accounting. The Court inquired if the $1,000.00 reserve is still needed. Ms. Woodbury stated that she probably should have left that out, that she does not expect anything else to be owed, that the petitioner has paid her already, that no taxes are owed, and that it would not need another court order for the $1,000.00. The Court noted the $1,000.00 reserve may be adjusted.

The Fourth Amendment to the Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Continued To Jan-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-29-2019 Continued To Dec-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jayleen J. Woodbury for Petitioner, Lydia Sheridan via Court Call.

The Court seeks clarification on what funds were sent back, what the funds were and whether it was estate funds. Ms. Woodbury stated that the funds are from a 401K account, that Vanguard would not give any information since her client was not the beneficiary, and that her client was estranged from her mother. Ms. Woodbury further stated that they sent the funds back to Vanguard, Vanguard sent the check back to Golden1, and received notification that the funds were deposited back into the account on October 15th. In addition, Ms. Woodbury stated her client has tried unsuccessfully to reach out to the people she thought could be the beneficiary, they can't trace whether the money belongs to the estate, they are not trying to claim it, Vanguard won't take it back, would like to move forward, and can't see any other option but to amend her petition to keep the money. The Second Amendment to the Report of Administrator and Petition for its Settlement, etc. is continued to December 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for 3rd amendment with as much information as possible and with notice given to people who are possible beneficiaries.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
